pm_decision_extract.decision_or_assessment: Surge-short entry decision for GV: 153% 5-day return on 19.5x volume with structural absence of corroborating narrative, severe fundamental deterioration, and distressed balance sheet.
pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: GV surged 153% in 5 days on 19.5x normal volume, triggering surge-short evaluation. Upstream agents (Narrative, Alt-Data Verify, Surge-Short) converge on a strong bear thesis: (1) STRUCTURAL ABSENCE OF NARRATIVE: zero news items pre-cutoff, zero 8-K filings in 90 days, zero insider Form 4 activity, zero analyst coverage. This absence is the primary bear signal—a legitimate surge catalyst would exhibit hard-to-fake corroboration in at least one channel. (2) FUNDAMENTAL DETERIORATION: net margin -314%, gross margin -25.6%, ROE -138.5%, debt-to-equity 9.46x, current ratio 0.066 (insolvency risk). Q2 2024 revenue $3.3M with -$5.9M net loss. (3) ALTERNATIVE DATA CONTRADICTION: Wikipedia pageviews show baseline attention (13 views on decision day, 8-27 range over 30 days), no abnormal spike. GitHub unmapped (not a software company or misconfigured). FMP sentiment endpoints all empty. (4) MOMENTUM WITHOUT FUNDAMENTALS: the surge appears driven by technical factors (short squeeze, retail attention, or pure momentum) rather than new information or operational improvement. (5) MACRO HEADWIND: Overheat regime with strict_quality_and_valuation_caution discipline; low-quality micro-caps face rotation risk. Risk agent confirms all hard-veto conditions pass (no sleeve breach, no lookahead violation, no critical data-quality flags). Surge-short sleeve cap is 10%; proposed position is 0.5% (well within cap). The preponderance of evidence supports a bear thesis: structural absence of expected corroboration relative to the magnitude of the price move is itself a signal that the surge is unmoored from fundamental or market-driven catalysts. Confidence is high; uncertainty is low.
invalidation_conditions: If a material news event or SEC filing occurred between 2025-03-06T09:30:00 and the actual price spike, and that event is not captured in the packet due to post-cutoff filtering, the 'no catalyst' assessment is invalidated.
If upstream agents provide evidence of a real, verifiable catalyst with corroborating SEC filing or credible news source, the bear thesis weakens.
If insider buying or institutional accumulation is revealed post-cutoff, confidence in short thesis would decline.
If subsequent earnings or 8-K disclosure reveals positive operational inflection (revenue growth, margin improvement, debt reduction), the thesis is invalidated.
If the stock reverses sharply on profit-taking within 1-2 trading days without further catalyst, the surge may be self-correcting and the short thesis becomes less urgent.
risk_flags: Micro-cap liquidity risk: 188M shares traded in one day; exit liquidity may be constrained on reversal.
Extreme leverage: debt-to-equity 9.46x; covenant breach risk if deterioration continues.
Insolvency risk: current ratio 0.066 indicates severe working-capital stress; liquidity crisis possible.
Negative gross margin (-25.6%): company is losing money on every unit sold; business model is broken.
No insider support: zero Form 4 activity in 90 days despite 153% surge suggests management does not believe in the move.
Macro headwind: Overheat regime with strict equity discipline; low-quality names face rotation risk.
Structural absence of narrative: zero news, zero filings, zero analyst coverage suggests momentum-driven surge, not fundamentals.
Post-cutoff news risk: 20 news items dropped post-cutoff; cannot rule out that a catalyst emerged after 09:30 ET decision window.
suggested_improvements: Obtain the 20 post-cutoff news items to determine if a material catalyst occurred between 2025-03-06T09:30:00 and the actual price spike.
Identify GV's sector and business model from SEC 10-K Item 1; apply sector-specific evidence expectations.
Monitor real-time social media (Twitter, Stocktwits, Reddit) to detect if retail meme narrative is driving the surge.
Track daily insider Form 4 filings post-decision; any insider buying would weaken the short thesis; any insider selling would strengthen it.
Obtain detailed debt schedule and covenant terms to assess refinancing risk and liquidity runway.
Monitor short-seller reports and activist research for any published bear theses on GV.
Set price-action alerts: if stock reverses >20% within 2 trading days, reassess momentum thesis; if it continues higher, monitor for short-squeeze risk.

pm_decision_extract.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet's news_event_summary block shows 20 items dropped post-cutoff, creating ambiguity about whether a material catalyst occurred at/after 09:30 ET. The opening-window decision mode (09:30-09:45 ET) is too narrow to capture intraday news flow. The fundamental_snapshot does not include sector/industry classification, limiting sector-aware reasoning. The alternative_data_features block shows all industry-specific adapters (software_devtools, semiconductor_patent, biotech_clinical, etc.) as not_connected, suggesting either GV is a micro-cap with minimal public footprint or the adapter selection logic failed. These gaps are noted but do not invalidate the bear thesis: the structural absence of expected evidence (news, filings, insider activity) relative to the magnitude of the price move is itself a signal.

pm_decision_extract.reason: GV exhibits 153% 5-day surge on 19.5x volume with zero corroborating narrative (no news, no 8-K, no insider activity, no analyst coverage). Fundamentals severely distressed: net margin -314%, gross margin -25.6%, debt-to-equity 9.46x, current ratio 0.066. Structural absence of hard-to-fake evidence relative to price move magnitude is bear-thesis signal. Momentum-driven surge on deteriorating balance sheet with no observable catalyst. All hard-veto conditions pass. Surge-short sleeve cap 10%; position 0.5% well within limit. Confidence high; uncertainty low. Recommend short entry at 0.5% per §4.8 fixed sizing.

Micro-cap liquidity and leverage risks noted; position sizing at 0.5% limits portfolio impact.

Post-cutoff news risk: 20 items dropped; recommend post-decision review of news published 09:30-16:00 ET on 2025-03-06.
Upstream agents (Narrative, Alt-Data Verify, Surge-Short, Risk) all converge on bear thesis; no conflicting signals.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: GV exhibits a 153.4% 5-day return with 19.5x relative volume spike (188M shares traded vs 9.7M 20-day average) on 2025-03-06 opening window. However, the evidence packet reveals a CRITICAL STRUCTURAL ABSENCE: news_event_summary.status='data_unavailable' with 0 items returned from polygon_news (7-day lookback) and 20 items DROPPED post-cutoff. No trigger_article, no catalyst_type, no catalyst_specificity_score populated. Filing_confirmation.status='not_evaluated' with no 8-K filings in last 90 days (sec_edgar returned 0 rows). No press release confirmation. Sentiment/ownership data not_evaluated. GitHub adapter failed (unmapped_ticker). FMP sentiment endpoints returned 0 rows. The only alternative data present is Wikipedia pageviews (13 views on 2025-03-06, baseline 8-27 range over 30 days) — a generic attention metric with no narrative specificity. Fundamental snapshot shows Q2 2024 data (accepted 2024-09-29): revenue $3.3M, net income -$5.9M, EPS -$1.59, net margin -175.69%. Valuation metrics are deeply negative (PE=-0.047, EV/EBITDA=-5.69, ROE=-1.38, net margin -314%). A 1:15 stock split occurred 2024-05-29. Corporate calendar shows 4 past earnings (most recent 2024-11-21 with EPS -$1.72) and no upcoming events within ±5 trading days. Macro regime is 'Overheat / Moderately Restrictive' (stress_score=2), which constrains equity allocation to 20% and mandates strict quality/valuation caution. SECTOR-AWARE REASONING: GV's actual sector is not explicitly stated in the packet. The business description is absent from fundamental_snapshot. Without sector identification, I cannot apply sector-specific evidence expectations (e.g., 'if biotech, expect FDA filings; if SaaS, expect developer activity'). This is a critical gap. The absence of news corroboration, SEC filings, insider activity, and sentiment data is STRUCTURAL and ASYMMETRIC relative to the magnitude of the price move. The company claim is UNKNOWN because no news trigger, press release, or official disclosure is present in the packet. The price action appears to be MOMENTUM-DRIVEN (high volume, no narrative anchor) or driven by an event that occurred AFTER the allowed_data_cutoff (20 news items dropped post-cutoff per news_event_summary.items_dropped_post_cutoff). Per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING and the UNIVERSAL SOFT-VETO PRINCIPLE, absence of expected corroboration relative to a 153% 5-day surge is itself a SIGNAL — specifically, a bear-thesis-leaning signal for surge_short candidates. The structural absence of news, filings, and sentiment data contradicts what a legitimate catalyst-driven move should exhibit. This pattern is consistent with either (a) a data collection artifact (news dropped post-cutoff, adapters failed), or (b) a genuine absence of fundamental catalyst, suggesting momentum/speculation/short-squeeze dynamics.
invalidation_conditions: If a material news event or SEC filing occurred between 2025-03-06T09:30:00 and the actual price spike, and that event is not captured in the packet due to post-cutoff filtering, the 'no catalyst' assessment is invalidated.
If GV is a micro-cap or shell company with minimal news coverage, the absence of news/filings is expected and does not signal fabrication.
If the 153% move is attributable to a short squeeze or gamma-driven options event (not captured in this packet), the momentum assessment is incomplete.
If Wikipedia pageviews spike is correlated with a specific catalyst (e.g., CEO announcement, product launch) not captured in news adapters, the assessment is incomplete.

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet's news_event_summary block is marked 'data_unavailable' with 20 items dropped post-cutoff. This is a critical architectural gap: the packet cannot distinguish between (a) genuinely no news pre-cutoff, and (b) news exists but was filtered out. The allowed_data_cutoff is 2025-03-06T09:30:00 (market open), but the price spike likely occurred during the first 15 minutes of trading (opening window). Any news released at 09:30:00 or immediately after would be dropped. The schema should either (1) include a 'pre_cutoff_news_count' field to confirm zero pre-cutoff items, or (2) expose the dropped items with a 'post_cutoff_reason' flag so the agent can assess whether the catalyst is genuinely absent or merely post-cutoff. Additionally, the fundamental_snapshot does not include sector/industry classification, which is essential for sector-aware reasoning per §6 SECTOR-AWARE REASONING SEQUENCE. The alternative_data_features block is largely not_connected or not_evaluated, reducing the agent's ability to corroborate the narrative via alternative signals.
recommended_followups: URGENT: Retrieve the 20 post-cutoff news items and assess whether any constitute a material catalyst (earnings miss, guidance cut, acquisition, bankruptcy, regulatory action, etc.).
URGENT: Identify GV's sector and business model from SEC 10-K or corporate profile; apply sector-specific evidence expectations.

HIGH: Retrieve full SEC Edgar filing history (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, S-1, etc.) to assess whether GV is a shell company, SPAC, or distressed entity.
HIGH: Assess whether the 1:15 stock split (2024-05-29) created a liquidity event or retail trading surge that persists into 2025-03-06.
MEDIUM: Retrieve press releases or investor relations announcements from company website or PR Newswire directly (not via FMP).
MEDIUM: Retrieve insider trading data (Form 4) for 6+ months pre-decision to assess accumulation/selling patterns.
MEDIUM: Cross-check Wikipedia pageviews spike (if any) against other attention metrics (Reddit, Twitter/X, search trends).
MEDIUM: Assess whether GV is a penny stock, micro-cap, or thinly traded security; if so, volume spikes and price moves may be driven by retail/speculative activity rather than fundamental catalysts.
LOW: Retrieve historical volatility and beta to assess whether the 153% move is consistent with GV's typical trading range.

alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: GV exhibits a 153% 5-day surge with 19.5x relative volume on 2025-03-06 opening window. The price dislocation is extreme and requires hard-to-fake corroboration. Alternative-data assessment reveals STRUCTURAL ABSENCE relative to what a legitimate surge candidate should produce: (1) Wikipedia pageviews show baseline attention (6-27 views/day, no spike pattern), consistent with a micro-cap with minimal public footprint; (2) Zero news items in 7-day window (polygon_news) and zero FMP news pre-cutoff; (3) Zero SEC filings in 90 days (no 8-K, no Form 4 insider activity); (4) All sentiment/ownership adapters unavailable or returned zero rows; (5) GitHub unmapped (not a software company or the adapter is misconfigured). Fundamental snapshot (Q2 2024, 5 months stale) shows severe distress: $3.3M revenue, $5.9M net loss, -175.69% net margin, 0.066 current ratio, 9.46 debt/equity. Valuation metrics are deeply negative (PE -0.047, EV/EBITDA -5.69). Macro regime is Overheat/Moderately Restrictive with strict_quality_and_valuation_caution discipline. The surge lacks ANY corroborating narrative catalyst (no news, no filings, no insider activity, no sentiment spike, no community attention). The absence of hard-to-fake evidence (SEC filings, news, insider transactions) relative to the magnitude of the price move is itself bear-thesis evidence for a surge_short candidate. The company appears to be a distressed micro-cap with minimal public disclosure footprint, and the 153% surge is unmoored from observable operational or market catalysts.

This suggests either GV is a micro-cap with minimal public footprint, or the adapter selection logic failed to identify the correct industry. The news_event_summary shows 20 items dropped post-cutoff, which may contain the actual catalyst; the opening-window decision mode (09:30-09:45 ET) is too narrow to capture intraday news flow. Recommend post-decision review of news published 09:30-16:00 ET on 2025-03-06.
recommended_followups: Post-decision: fetch news published 09:30-16:00 ET on 2025-03-06 to identify catalyst
Confirm GV's sector and business model from 10-K or latest press release
Monitor for 8-K filing within 4 business days of surge (material event disclosure)
Check for insider Form 4 transactions (buys/sells) in days following surge
Assess if surge is driven by short squeeze, retail meme attention, or legitimate catalyst
Review post-decision price action: if volume declines and price reverts, supports bear thesis
alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: GV exhibits a 153% 5-day surge with 19.5x relative volume, but alternative-data evidence shows STRUCTURAL ABSENCE of corroborating catalysts. Wikipedia pageviews show baseline attention (no spike), zero news in 7-day window, zero SEC filings in 90 days, zero insider activity, and all sentiment/ownership adapters unavailable or empty. The magnitude of the price move is unmoored from observable hard-to-fake evidence (news, filings, insider transactions, regulatory approvals). For a surge_short candidate, this structural absence relative to the public narrative is bear-thesis evidence: the surge lacks fundamental or market-driven justification and is likely driven by technical factors (short squeeze, retail attention, or momentum) rather than new information. The company's severe balance-sheet distress (current ratio 0.066, debt/equity 9.46, net margin -175.69%) and macro regime restrictive for low-quality equities further support a high narrative-price gap.
alt_data_verify.adapter_selection_reason: wikipedia_pageviews is the only alternative-data adapter with rows returned (30 rows, 30-day window). All industry-specific adapters (software_devtools, semiconductor_patent, biotech_clinical, consumer_traffic, bank_regulatory, airline_travel, energy_commodity, auto_mobility) are not_connected. GV's sector is not identified in the packet; adapter selection defaulted to wikipedia_pageviews as a fallback. This is a limitation: without sector identification, cannot apply sector-aware reasoning for expected alt-data footprint.
alt_data_verify.hard_to_fake_signal_summary: STRUCTURAL ABSENCE of hard-to-fake evidence is the primary bear-thesis signal: (1) Zero SEC filings (8-K, Form 4) in 90 days — no material event disclosure, no insider activity; (2) Zero news items in 7-day window (polygon_news) — no public catalyst; (3) Wikipedia pageviews show baseline attention (6-27 views/day), no spike pattern — inconsistent with meme-stock or retail-driven narrative; (4) All sentiment/ownership adapters unavailable or empty — cannot assess market positioning; (5) Fundamental snapshot (Q2 2024) shows severe distress (net margin -175.69%, current ratio 0.066, debt/equity 9.46) — no operational improvement to justify surge. The absence of corroborating hard-to-fake evidence relative to the magnitude of the price move (153% in 5 days) is itself evidence that the surge is not driven by new information or operational improvement.
industry_specific_evidence_used: corporate_calendar.splits (1:15 on 2024-05-29) — reverse split, typical of distressed micro-caps

surge_short.decision_or_assessment: Surge-short evaluation for GV: 153% 5-day return on 19.5x normal volume. Structural absence of corroborating narrative evidence combined with severe fundamental deterioration supports bear thesis.
surge_short.reasoning_summary: GV surged 153% in 5 days on 19.5x normal volume, triggering surge_short evaluation. The packet contains NO corroborating narrative evidence: zero news items published <= cutoff, zero 8-K filings in 90 days, zero insider Form 4 activity in 90 days, zero GitHub presence (unmapped ticker), zero FMP sentiment data. This structural absence is the primary bear-thesis signal. Simultaneously, fundamental metrics are severely distressed: net margin -314%, gross margin -25.6%, ROE -138.5%, debt-to-equity 9.46x, current ratio 0.066 (insolvency risk). The company reported Q2 2024 revenue of $3.3M with -$5.9M net loss. Wikipedia pageviews (13 on decision day) show no abnormal attention spike. The surge appears to be pure momentum on a distressed, unprofitable, highly leveraged micro-cap with no observable catalyst, no recent SEC disclosure, and no insider support. The absence of news, filings, and insider activity is itself the bear signal: a legitimate company surging on a real catalyst would have observable corroboration in at least one of these channels. The macro regime (Overheat / Moderately Restrictive, stress_score=2) adds caution to low-quality equity positions. Valuation is not justified by fundamentals; the surge is momentum-driven on a deteriorating balance sheet.
invalidation_conditions: If upstream agents (Narrative, Alt-Data Verification, Fund/Net/Val) provide evidence of a real, verifiable catalyst (e.g., partnership, product launch, regulatory approval) with corroborating SEC filing or credible news source, the bear thesis weakens.
If insider buying or institutional accumulation is revealed in Form 4 or 13F data post-cutoff, confidence in short thesis would decline.
If subsequent earnings or 8-K disclosure reveals positive operational inflection (revenue growth, margin improvement, debt reduction), the thesis is invalidated.
If the stock reverses sharply on profit-taking within 1-2 trading days without further catalyst, the surge may be self-correcting and the short thesis becomes less urgent.
risk_flags: Micro-cap liquidity risk: 188M shares traded in one day; exit liquidity may be constrained on reversal.
Extreme leverage: debt-to-equity 9.46x; any further deterioration could trigger covenant breach or restructuring.
Insolvency risk: current ratio 0.066 indicates severe working-capital stress; company may face liquidity crisis.
Negative gross margin (-25.6%): company is losing money on every unit sold; business model is broken.
No insider support: zero Form 4 activity in 90 days despite 153% surge suggests management does not believe in the move.
Macro headwind: Overheat regime with strict equity discipline; low-quality names face rotation risk.
Structural absence of narrative: zero news, zero filings, zero analyst coverage suggests market is pricing on pure momentum, not fundamentals.
suggested_improvements: Obtain real-time news feed (Twitter, Stocktwits, Reddit) to detect if retail meme narrative is driving the surge; if so, short thesis strengthens (momentum without fundamentals).
Cross-check with short-seller reports or activist research to see if any bear thesis has been published; absence of bear research on a distressed micro-cap is itself a signal.
Monitor insider Form 4 filings daily post-decision; any insider buying would weaken the short thesis; any insider selling would strengthen it.
Track debt maturity schedule and covenant compliance; if company is near refinancing, liquidity crisis risk is acute.
Obtain detailed revenue breakdown by customer/product; $3.3M quarterly revenue on a $1B+ market cap (at surge price) is extreme and suggests either data error or massive dilution.
Verify stock split history: corporate_calendar shows 1:15 split on 2024-05-29; ensure all historical prices are adjusted correctly.

surge_short.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The packet lacks upstream_agent_outputs (Narrative, Alt-Data Verification, Fund/Net/Val verdicts), which would normally provide cross-validation of the bear thesis. The surge_short agent is designed to run in a multi-agent pipeline where prior agents populate narrative_price_gap_assessment and alternative_data_features with LLM reasoning. In this case, narrative_price_gap_assessment.status='not_evaluated' and alternative_data_features.adapter_selection.adapter_confidence='not_evaluated', indicating the prelude agents have not yet run or their outputs are not included in the packet. The surge_short agent must therefore reason from raw evidence blocks alone. This is defensible under §13.6 SOFT_REASONING and the universal-soft-veto principle: structural absence of expected evidence (news, filings, insider activity) is itself a bear-thesis signal for a surge candidate.
recommended_followups: Obtain upstream agent outputs (Narrative, Alt-Data Verification, Fund/Net/Val) to cross-validate the bear thesis.
Monitor real-time social media and retail sentiment to determine if the surge is driven by meme/retail narrative or by institutional accumulation.
Request detailed business description from latest 10-K (Item 1) to understand what GV actually does and why revenue is so low relative to market cap.
Obtain detailed debt schedule and covenant terms to assess refinancing risk and liquidity runway.
Track daily insider Form 4 filings and institutional 13F positioning post-decision to detect smart-money support or abandonment.
Monitor short-seller reports and activist research for any published bear theses on GV.
Set price-action alerts: if stock reverses >20% within 2 trading days, reassess momentum thesis; if it continues higher, monitor for short-squeeze risk.
surge_short.company_claim: No verifiable claim found in packet. Surge appears to be momentum-driven on unknown catalyst.
surge_short.hold_review_exit_reason: Initial short entry on surge_short sleeve. Position to be reviewed if: (1) stock reverses >20% within 2 trading days (momentum exhaustion), (2) upstream agents provide evidence of real catalyst (invalidates bear thesis), (3) insider buying or institutional accumulation detected (smart-money support), (4) company announces material positive development (earnings beat, partnership, product launch). Exit triggers: stop-loss at +50% from entry (risk management on micro-cap volatility); profit-taking at -50% from entry (momentum reversal target).

surge_short.audit_rationale: GV surged 153% in 5 days on 19.5x volume with zero corroborating narrative evidence (no news, no 8-K, no insider activity, no analyst coverage). Fundamentals are severely distressed: net margin -314%, gross margin -25.6%, ROE -138.5%, debt-to-equity 9.46x, current ratio 0.066. Structural absence of expected SEC/news/insider footprint is the primary bear signal. Momentum-driven surge on deteriorating balance sheet with no observable catalyst. Short thesis is valid; evidence is sufficient; confidence is high. Recommend short at 0.5% initial position per v0.4 sleeve discipline.

pm.decision_or_assessment: Surge-short entry decision for GV: 153% 5-day return on 19.5x volume with structural absence of corroborating narrative, severe fundamental deterioration, and distressed balance sheet.
pm.reasoning_summary: GV surged 153% in 5 days on 19.5x normal volume, triggering surge-short evaluation. Upstream agents (Narrative, Alt-Data Verify, Surge-Short) converge on a strong bear thesis: (1) STRUCTURAL ABSENCE OF NARRATIVE: zero news items pre-cutoff, zero 8-K filings in 90 days, zero insider Form 4 activity, zero analyst coverage. This absence is the primary bear signal—a legitimate surge catalyst would exhibit hard-to-fake corroboration in at least one channel. (2) FUNDAMENTAL DETERIORATION: net margin -314%, gross margin -25.6%, ROE -138.5%, debt-to-equity 9.46x, current ratio 0.066 (insolvency risk). Q2 2024 revenue $3.3M with -$5.9M net loss. (3) ALTERNATIVE DATA CONTRADICTION: Wikipedia pageviews show baseline attention (13 views on decision day, 8-27 range over 30 days), no abnormal spike. GitHub unmapped (not a software company or misconfigured). FMP sentiment endpoints all empty. (4) MOMENTUM WITHOUT FUNDAMENTALS: the surge appears driven by technical factors (short squeeze, retail attention, or pure momentum) rather than new information or operational improvement. (5) MACRO HEADWIND: Overheat regime with strict_quality_and_valuation_caution discipline; low-quality micro-caps face rotation risk. Risk agent confirms all hard-veto conditions pass (no sleeve breach, no lookahead violation, no critical data-quality flags). Surge-short sleeve cap is 10%; proposed position is 0.5% (well within cap). The preponderance of evidence supports a bear thesis: structural absence of expected corroboration relative to the magnitude of the price move is itself a signal that the surge is unmoored from fundamental or market-driven catalysts. Confidence is high; uncertainty is low.
